visuals.alt: Base ten blocks showing the result: 5 tens and 9 ones = 59

steps.explanation: Start with the ones place. 5 ones plus 4 ones equals 9 ones.

steps.instruction: Put the tens and ones together.
steps.explanation: 50 + 9 = 59. When the ones don't add up to more than 9, there is no regrouping needed.
13 ones is more than 9, so we regroup: 13 = 1 ten + 3 ones. Write 3 in the ones place and carry 1 ten.

steps.subgoalLabel: Add the tens (plus the carried ten)
steps.explanation: Don't forget the extra ten from regrouping! 40 + 20 + 10 = 70.

steps.instruction: Put the tens and ones together.
steps.explanation: 46 + 27 = 73. Regrouping (carrying) is needed when the ones add up to 10 or more.
